menu_options.cloud: Sign in with Inkbird email + password
menu_options.google: Sign in with Google (paste idToken or Google ID)

cloud.title: Inkbird account login
cloud.description: Sign in with the email and password you use in the Inkbird app.

data.country_code: Country code (e.g. 1 for US, 34 for Spain, 44 for UK)

google.title: Google-bridged Inkbird account
google.description:
Paste your **Google account ID** (the 21-digit numeric `sub`). Two ways to get it without exposing your idToken:

**A. OAuth Playground → userinfo (recommended, easiest):**
1. Open https://developers.google.com/oauthplayground
2. In Step 1, expand **Google OAuth2 API v2** and tick `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/userinfo.profile`.
3. Click **Authorize APIs**, sign in with the Google account you use for Inkbird.
4. In Step 2, click **Exchange authorization code for tokens**.
5. In Step 3, change the request URL to `https://www.googleapis.com/oauth2/v2/userinfo` and click **Send the request**.
6. The response contains `"id": "123456789..."` — that 21-digit number is your `sub`. Copy it here.

**B. Paste a full idToken instead** (we extract `sub` locally and discard the rest):
- Same steps 1–4 above. The response from step 4 includes an `id_token` field — paste the whole JWT.

**⚠️ Security note:** Inkbird's backend does NOT validate Google idTokens — it trusts whoever supplies a `sub`. Treat your `sub` like a password: anyone who learns it can log into your Inkbird/Tuya account. Full details + how this integration handles it: see README.md → "Security & privacy".

data.country_code: Country code (e.g. 1 for US, 34 for Spain, 44 for UK)

confirm.title: Confirm Device
confirm.description:
Found **{name}** ({online})
- Device ID: `{device_id}`
- {lan_status}

The integration will prefer direct LAN polling when reachable, and fall back to Tuya cloud polling otherwise. Press Submit to add the integration.

error.cannot_connect: Login failed — check credentials, region, or country code.
error.invalid_token: Couldn't parse a Google sub from that value.
error.no_devices: No devices found on this account.

abort.no_devices: No devices found on this account.
abort.unsupported_product: Your account has device(s), but none of them are supported by this integration yet. Check the Home Assistant log for the Tuya `productId` and open a GitHub issue requesting support.
